behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is command-to-artifact-store wiring (stage output mapped to artifact key). A pure unit test of one helper can miss real misbinding between evaluate flow and artifact persistence. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess required; no AI/ML output-quality judgment; no multi-agentic chain. Rejection gate: rejected Agentic because behavior text is storage destination only (artifact slot routing), not model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate-stage with fixed deterministic inputs and an in-process artifact store in a real run context; assert a `grounding` artifact is created/updated and `plan` is not written by this stage.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module orchestration: implement flow must read one artifact and write another without regression. Unit tests for read/write helpers alone can miss wiring and state handoff errors. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess needed; correctness here is artifact plumbing, not evaluating model quality; single stage, not multi-agentic chain. Rejection gate: rejected Agentic because behavior text scopes to read/write behavior, not whether model output is good.
behaviors.test_description: Seed run context with a `grounding` artifact, execute implement flow with deterministic stubs for generation, and assert implement consumes grounding context while persisting final output only to `plan`.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: Bug appears at interaction boundary between issue-content diagnosis detection and artifact-write side effects. A local function test may miss that deep-dive command still calls plan writer indirectly. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess; no required model-quality assertion; not a multi-agentic sequence. Rejection gate: rejected Agentic because behavior text is side-effect suppression (`no longer overwrites plan`), not correctness of deep-dive reasoning quality.
behaviors.test_description: Provide issue content containing existing diagnosis, run deep-dive stage in-process with real stage wiring, and assert no `plan` overwrite/write occurs while other expected outputs remain intact.

behaviors.description: Artifact lineage is preserved so implement can read the `grounding` artifact produced earlier in the same run context.
behaviors.justification: Lineage preservation is state propagation across modules (artifact IDs/context, run-scoped storage, retrieval path). Unit tests cannot reliably catch broken handoff metadata across boundaries. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess required; no AI/ML output-quality dependency; single cross-stage handoff validation, not full multi-agentic chain. Rejection gate: rejected Workflow because behavior text requires lineage/handoff preservation, not multiple real model decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute evaluate then implement within one in-process run context, verify implement resolves and reads the exact prior `grounding` artifact via lineage metadata, and confirm no cross-run leakage.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires a full multi-step agent pipeline and verifies downstream output reflects earlier grounding. Real failure can occur only across sequential agentic handoffs, where each step’s decision affects next-step context usage. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly demands full chain execution and end output reflection across steps, which disqualifies a single wiring-only integration test.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real end-to-end chain (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`) in one run context, inject a distinctive grounding signal early, and assert the final implementation plan incorporates that signal while artifacts remain correctly separated.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: This is fallback orchestration and artifact-store integrity across modules under missing-input conditions. Unit tests of fallback helper logic may miss corruption caused by persistence-layer interaction. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess needed; no model-quality requirement; no multi-agentic chain required. Rejection gate: rejected Agentic because behavior text focuses on safe fallback and artifact integrity, not quality of generated content.
behaviors.test_description: Start with run context lacking `grounding` but containing other artifacts, execute implement flow, and assert: `plan` is emitted, existing unrelated artifacts are unchanged, and no invalid artifact mutations occur.
